channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigeria begins court martial of 36 officers over alleged coup plot
item.description: Nigeria's government faces mounting pressure as 36 senior military officers stand trial before a special court martial over an alleged 2025 coup plot, raising concerns about the country's worsening security situation.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish defense firms secure $8 billion in export deals at SAHA Expo
item.description: Turkish defense companies signed nearly $8 billion in export contracts during the first three days of SAHA Expo, a major defense trade show, highlighting Turkey's growing role as a defense exporter.

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain expels three MPs after they voted against royal citizenship decree
item.description: Bahrain has revoked the parliamentary membership of three lawmakers just days after dozens had their nationalities stripped in connection with the Iran war context, amid a widening crackdown on dissent.

item.title: 🛢️ Iran seizes oil tanker Ocean Koi in Gulf of Oman
item.description: Iran's navy seized an oil tanker named Ocean Koi in the Gulf of Oman over an alleged attempt to disrupt Iran's oil exports, Iranian state media reported, adding to escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🏥 WHO documents over 3,000 attacks on Ukraine healthcare since war began
item.description: The World Health Organization has registered more than 3,000 attacks on Ukraine's healthcare system since the start of the war, marking one of the highest numbers of attacks on healthcare in any conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇪 UAE intercepts drone and missile attack as Iran war ceasefire frays
item.description: The United Arab Emirates said it intercepted missiles and drones as tensions escalate despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations between Iran and the US. The UAE Ministry of Defense said its air defenses were "actively engaging" incoming threats.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea to deploy artillery targeting Seoul, commission first destroyer
item.description: North Korea announced it will deploy new long-range artillery systems capable of striking Seoul this year and commission its first naval destroyer. Leader Kim Jong Un inspected weapons production as the country continues military expansion amid stalled denuclearization talks.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia and Ukraine accuse each other of violating Victory Day ceasefire
item.description: Both sides reported hundreds of ceasefire violations as Russia celebrates its World War II victory with a muted parade. Moscow will have no military hardware on display for the first time in nearly two decades, while Ukraine continues drone strikes deep inside Russian territory.

item.title: ⚖️ US trade court rules Trump 10% global tariff illegal
item.description: A panel of federal judges on the Court of International Trade blocked President Trump's 10% tariffs on most US imports, ruling in a 2-1 decision that the law sidestepped a Supreme Court ruling that struck down blanket tariffs imposed under emergency powers.

item.title: ✈️ US jet fuel shipments could ease European shortages amid Iran war
item.description: Aviation experts say European airlines could use US-made jet fuel to combat soaring prices and limited supply caused by the Iran war. The International Air Transport Association warned fuel shortages may emerge if the Middle East conflict continues.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Secret document reveals Russia's plans to supply advanced weapons to Iran
item.description: A classified document obtained by The Economist shows Russia offered to provide Iran with innovative weapons in large enough quantities to inflict significant casualties on American and allied forces, beyond the intelligence support Moscow is already believed to have provided.

item.title: 🇪🇺 European countries emerge as NATO leaders as U.S. role recedes
item.description: President Trump decision to leave NATO in the dark before launching strikes on Iran has inflamed tensions, pushing European leaders to consider a future where the U.S. no longer leads the alliance.

item.title: 🌋 Indonesia volcano eruption kills three hikers on remote island
item.description: Mount Dukono erupted on Halmahera Island, killing three hikers including two Singaporeans. Rescuers evacuated 14 climbers while searching for others who defied safety restrictions.

item.title: 🇨🇩 Islamic State-affiliated group kills 40 in Congo near Uganda border
item.description: Allied Democratic Forces attacked villages in eastern Congo overnight, killing at least 40 and burning homes. The group pledged allegiance to IS in 2019 and has long operated in the border region.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon civil defense member killed in Israeli strike amid ceasefire
item.description: An Israeli strike in southern Lebanon killed a civil defense rescuer despite the ongoing truce. WHO has verified 152 attacks on healthcare in Lebanon since the war began, with 103 deaths.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A Chinese-owned oil products tanker was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, according to Chinese media, as the US launched and then suspended a plan to help stranded vessels transiting the vital waterway.

item.title: ⚔️ US carries out retaliatory strikes on Iran after Hormuz clash
item.description: The US military conducted retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets it said were responsible for attacking American forces, calling the hostilities "unprovoked" amid renewed fighting in the Gulf region.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China sees weakened US deterrence on Taiwan as Iran war drains weapons
item.description: Chinese analysts say America"s ability to deter a war over Taiwan is weakened by the Iran conflict, giving Beijing leverage ahead of President Trump"s upcoming summit with Xi Jinping.

item.title: 🇦🇪🇵🇸 UAE sends $100 million to train new Gaza police force under Trump-backed plan
item.description: The UAE has transferred $100 million to Trump's Board of Peace to fund training of a 27,000-strong Gaza police force composed of Jordanian and Egyptian-trained officers, marking a significant step in post-war governance planning.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇵🇰🇮🇳 China's J-35 stealth fighters to give Pakistan edge over India in fifth-generation tech
item.description: Pakistan is set to acquire Chinese J-35 stealth jets, potentially giving it a technological advantage over India which lacks fifth-generation fighters despite having a larger overall fleet.

item.title: 🇩🇪💻 German cyber chief warns: 'We are not at war, but we're not at peace either'
item.description: Germany's cyber warfare commander says allied nations face relentless digital attacks from Russia and other adversaries, with cyber intrusions increasingly targeting defense industries as European rearmament accelerates.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇭🎯 US-Philippine Balikatan drills expand with more nations and advanced missiles
item.description: American and Filipino forces wrapped up annual exercises featuring troops from five additional nations and deployed advanced systems including NMESIS and HIMARS, demonstrating deepening Indo-Pacific security cooperation.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇸🇪 Ukraine-Sweden Gripen Fighter Deal Could Be Signed Within Months
item.description: Ukraine's defense minister says talks for Saab-made Gripen fighter jets are progressing well, with a potential deal signed within months during his visit to Stockholm.

item.title: 🇰🇵⚔️ North Korea Deploys New Artillery Systems Capable of Striking Seoul
item.description: Kim Jong Un inspected new long-range artillery with ranges exceeding 60km to be deployed at the South Korean border, bolstering ability to hit Seoul, state media reports.

item.title: 💰🛢️ $7 Billion in Well-Timed Oil Bets Before Iran War News Sparks Scrutiny
item.description: Legal experts and lawmakers are calling for investigations into whether well-timed oil trades before the Iran war news were based on inside information or leaks.
